frameworks.name: Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: Perception widely framed as Bayesian feature inference.

frameworks.name: Holistic inference
frameworks.one_line: Inference operates across all levels of the representational hierarchy.

frameworks.name: Holistic matching model
frameworks.one_line: Optimal match across feature and higher-level category representations.

frameworks.name: Bayesian observer models
frameworks.one_line: Prior single-level Bayesian models fail on some response patterns.

core_question: Does orientation perception reflect inference across multiple levels of the representational hierarchy rather than only at the feature level?
key_assumptions: Perception is a probabilistic inference process
Stimuli have both feature-level (orientation) and higher-level (category) representations
Subjects' adjustment responses reflect an optimal joint match
manipulations: Test stimulus orientation in method-of-adjustment matching tasks
Reanalysis across multiple existing psychophysical datasets
key_findings: Holistic matching model quantitatively fits subjects' response behavior
Model accounts for response patterns previous Bayesian models fail to capture even qualitatively
interpretations: Orientation perception is a holistic inference across feature and category levels
Single-level Bayesian feature inference is insufficient to explain perceptual matching behavior
